name: Infant tongue cannot say full names
source_quote: my infant tongue could make of both names nothing longer or more explicit than Pip.

name: Pip names himself Pip
source_quote: So, I, called myself Pip

name: Others come to call him Pip
source_quote: and came to be called Pip.

name: Pip reports father’s family name as Pirrip
source_quote: I give Pirrip as my father’s family name, on the authority of his tombstone

name: Pip’s sister marries the blacksmith
source_quote: my sister,—Mrs. Joe Gargery, who married the blacksmith.

name: Pip has never seen his father
source_quote: As I never saw my father
time: throughout Pip’s life up to then

name: Pip has never seen his mother
source_quote: or my mother
time: throughout Pip’s life up to then

name: Pip has never seen any likeness of his parents
source_quote: and never saw any likeness of either of them

name: Pip has unreasonable fancies about parents’ appearance from tombstones
source_quote: my first fancies regarding what they were like were unreasonably derived from their tombstones.

name: Pip interprets shape of letters to imagine father as square, stout, dark with curly hair
source_quote: The shape of the letters on my father’s, gave me an odd idea that he was a square, stout, dark man, with curly black hair.

name: Pip reads or notes the inscription about Georgiana
source_quote: From the character and turn of the inscription, “Also Georgiana Wife of the Above,”

name: Pip draws childish conclusion that mother was freckled and sickly
source_quote: I drew a childish conclusion that my mother was freckled and sickly.

name: Five little brothers die young (give up trying to get a living)
source_quote: five little brothers of mine,—who gave up trying to get a living, exceedingly early in that universal struggle

name: The five brothers are commemorated by five little stone lozenges
source_quote: To five little stone lozenges... which were arranged in a neat row beside their grave, and were sacred to the memory of five little brothers of mine

name: Pip forms belief about brothers’ posture and pockets at birth
source_quote: I am indebted for a belief I religiously entertained that they had all been born on their backs with their hands in their trousers-pockets

name: Pip religiously entertains this belief for some time
source_quote: I am indebted for a belief I religiously entertained

name: Pip recognizes his home region as marsh country by the river
source_quote: Ours was the marsh country, down by the river
location: the marsh country, down by the river

name: Pip estimates distance from river to sea as twenty miles
source_quote: within, as the river wound, twenty miles of the sea.

name: Pip experiences first vivid and broad impression of the identity of things
source_quote: My first most vivid and broad impression of the identity of things seems to me to have been gained on a memorable raw afternoon towards evening.
time: a memorable raw afternoon towards evening in childhood

name: Narrator realizes place is a churchyard
source_quote: I found out for certain that this bleak place overgrown with nettles was the churchyard
location: bleak place overgrown with nettles / churchyard

name: Pip understands Philip Pirrip is dead and buried
source_quote: Philip Pirrip, late of this parish ... were dead and buried

name: Pip understands Georgiana is dead and buried
source_quote: and also Georgiana wife of the above, were dead and buried

name: Pip understands Alexander, Bartholomew, Abraham, Tobias, and Roger are dead and buried
source_quote: Alexander, Bartholomew, Abraham, Tobias, and Roger, infant children of the aforesaid, were also dead and buried

name: Pip recognizes dark flat wilderness as marshes
source_quote: the dark flat wilderness beyond the churchyard ... was the marshes
location: dark flat wilderness beyond the churchyard

name: Pip notices marshes are intersected with dikes and mounds and gates
source_quote: intersected with dikes and mounds and gates

name: Pip perceives scattered cattle feeding on the marshes
source_quote: with scattered cattle feeding on it

name: Pip recognizes the low leaden line as the river
source_quote: and that the low leaden line beyond was the river
location: low leaden line beyond the marshes

name: Pip recognizes distant savage lair as the sea
source_quote: and that the distant savage lair from which the wind was rushing was the sea

name: Pip recognizes himself as the small bundle of shivers
source_quote: and that the small bundle of shivers growing afraid of it all and beginning to cry, was Pip

name: Pip grows afraid of the surroundings
source_quote: the small bundle of shivers growing afraid of it all

name: Pip begins to cry
source_quote: small bundle of shivers ... beginning to cry

name: Terrible voice orders Pip to hold his noise
source_quote: “Hold your noise!” cried a terrible voice
time: immediately after Pip begins to cry
location: among the graves at the side of the church porch

name: Man starts up from among the graves
source_quote: as a man started up from among the graves
location: among the graves at the side of the church porch

name: Man threatens to cut Pip’s throat
source_quote: “Keep still, you little devil, or I’ll cut your throat!”

name: Narrator describes the man’s clothing and iron on leg
source_quote: A fearful man, all in coarse grey, with a great iron on his leg

name: Pip notices man has no hat, broken shoes, and rag around head
source_quote: A man with no hat, and with broken shoes, and with an old rag tied round his head

name: Pip recognizes the man has been soaked, smothered, lamed, cut, stung, and torn
source_quote: A man who had been soaked in water, and smothered in mud, and lamed by stones, and cut by flints, and stung by nettles, and torn by briars

name: Pip perceives the man limping
source_quote: who limped

name: Pip perceives the man shivering
source_quote: and shivered

name: Pip perceives the man glaring
source_quote: and glared

name: Pip perceives the man growling
source_quote: and growled

name: Pip notices the man’s teeth chattering
source_quote: whose teeth chattered in his head

name: Man seizes Pip by the chin
source_quote: as he seized me by the chin

name: Pip pleads for his throat not to be cut
source_quote: “Oh! Don’t cut my throat, sir,” I pleaded in terror. “Pray don’t do it, sir.”

name: Pip experiences terror
source_quote: I pleaded in terror

name: Man speaks again, saying 'Once more'
source_quote: “Once more,” said the man

name: Man stares at Pip
source_quote: said the man, staring at me

name: Man commands Pip to 'Give it mouth'
source_quote: “Give it mouth!”

name: Man orders child to show where he lives
source_quote: “Show us where you live,” said the man. “Pint out the place!”

name: Child indicates location of village
source_quote: I pointed to where our village lay, on the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards
location: where our village lay, on the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards, a mile or more from the church

name: Man pauses to inspect the child
source_quote: The man, after looking at me for a moment
location: where our village lay, on the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards, a mile or more from the church

name: Man overturns the child
source_quote: turned me upside down
time: after looking at me for a moment
location: where our village lay, on the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards, a mile or more from the church

name: Man empties child’s pockets
source_quote: and emptied my pockets
time: immediately after turning me upside down
location: where our village lay, on the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards, a mile or more from the church

name: Narrator notes only a piece of bread in pockets
source_quote: There was nothing in them but a piece of bread.
location: where our village lay, on the flat in-shore among the alder-trees and pollards, a mile or more from the church

name: Narrator experiences disorientation at church seeming inverted
source_quote: he made it go head over heels before me, and I saw the steeple under my feet
time: when the man turns him upside down

name: Church metaphorically ‘comes to itself’ for the narrator
source_quote: When the church came to itself,—for he was so sudden and strong
time: When the church came to itself

name: Narrator is seated on a high tombstone
source_quote: I was seated on a high tombstone
time: when the church came to itself

name: Narrator trembles in fear
source_quote: trembling while he ate the bread ravenously

name: Man eats the bread ravenously
source_quote: while he ate the bread ravenously

name: Man verbally abuses the child, calling him a young dog
source_quote: “You young dog,” said the man

name: Man licks his lips after eating
source_quote: said the man, licking his lips
time: as he speaks to the child

name: Man comments on the child’s fat cheeks
source_quote: “what fat cheeks you ha’ got.”

name: Narrator expresses belief about his own fat cheeks
source_quote: I believe they were fat

name: Narrator states he was undersized for his years
source_quote: though I was at that time undersized for my years

name: Narrator states he was not strong
source_quote: and not strong.

name: Man threatens to eat the boy
source_quote: “Darn me if I couldn’t eat ’em,” said the man, with a threatening shake of his head, “and if I han’t half a mind to’t!”

name: Boy hopes the man won’t eat him
source_quote: I earnestly expressed my hope that he wouldn’t

name: Boy expresses his hope verbally
source_quote: I earnestly expressed my hope that he wouldn’t

name: Boy tightens his grip on the tombstone
source_quote: and held tighter to the tombstone on which he had put me

name: Boy tries to keep himself on the tombstone
source_quote: partly, to keep myself upon it

name: Boy tries to keep himself from crying
source_quote: partly, to keep myself from crying

name: Man demands to know where the boy’s mother is
source_quote: “Now lookee here!” said the man. “Where’s your mother?”

name: Man starts suddenly
source_quote: He started

name: Man makes a short run
source_quote: made a short run

name: Man stops after running
source_quote: and stopped

name: Man looks over his shoulder
source_quote: and looked over his shoulder

name: Boy timidly explains where his mother is
source_quote: “There, sir!” I timidly explained. “Also Georgiana. That’s my mother.”

name: Boy identifies Georgiana as his mother
source_quote: “Also Georgiana. That’s my mother.”

name: Boy feels timidity while speaking
source_quote: “There, sir!” I timidly explained.

name: Man speaks exclamation on returning
source_quote: “Oh!” said he, coming back.
location: unspecified nearby place (where he comes back)

name: Man returns to the narrator
source_quote: said he, coming back.
location: place where the narrator is (near the graves)

name: Man asks if those are the narrator's parents
source_quote: “And is that your father alonger your mother?”
location: near the narrator and the graves

name: Narrator confirms parents and adds detail
source_quote: “Yes, sir,” said I; “him too; late of this parish.”
time: immediately after being asked about parents

name: Man mutters and considers
source_quote: “Ha!” he muttered then, considering.

name: Man asks who narrator lives with, threatening life
source_quote: “Who d’ye live with,—supposin’ you’re kindly let to live, which I han’t made up my mind about?”

name: Man expresses uncertainty about letting boy live
source_quote: “supposin’ you’re kindly let to live, which I han’t made up my mind about”

name: Narrator states he lives with his sister and identifies her
source_quote: “My sister, sir,—Mrs. Joe Gargery,—wife of Joe Gargery, the blacksmith, sir.”

name: Narrator identifies Joe Gargery as a blacksmith
source_quote: “wife of Joe Gargery, the blacksmith, sir.”
time: within same reply about his sister

name: Man repeats and acknowledges blacksmith information
source_quote: “Blacksmith, eh?” said he.
time: after hearing Joe is a blacksmith

name: Man looks down at his leg
source_quote: And looked down at his leg.

name: Man looks darkly at his leg and Pip
source_quote: After darkly looking at his leg and me several times

name: Man comes closer to the tombstone
source_quote: he came closer to my tombstone

name: Man takes Pip by both arms
source_quote: took me by both arms

name: Man tilts Pip back to his limit
source_quote: and tilted me back as far as he could hold me
time: immediately after taking him by the arms

name: Man looks powerfully down into Pip’s eyes
source_quote: so that his eyes looked most powerfully down into mine

name: Pip looks helplessly up at the man
source_quote: and mine looked most helplessly up into his

name: Man begins speaking with warning about life
source_quote: “Now lookee here,” he said, “the question being whether you’re to be let to live.

name: Man asks if Pip knows what a file is
source_quote: You know what a file is?”

name: Man tilts Pip further after each question
source_quote: After each question he tilted me over a little more, so as to give me a greater sense of helplessness and danger.

name: Man orders Pip to get him a file
source_quote: “You get me a file.”

name: Man tilts Pip again after ordering the file
source_quote: He tilted me again.
time: immediately after the command about the file

name: Man orders Pip to get him wittles
source_quote: “And you get me wittles.”

name: Man tilts Pip again after ordering wittles
source_quote: He tilted me again.
time: immediately after the command about wittles

name: Man orders Pip to bring both file and wittles
source_quote: “You bring ’em both to me.”

name: Man tilts Pip again after ordering both
source_quote: He tilted me again.
time: immediately after saying bring them both

name: Man threatens to take Pip’s heart and liver out
source_quote: “Or I’ll have your heart and liver out.”
time: after commanding Pip to bring both items

name: Man tilts Pip again after making lethal threat
source_quote: He tilted me again.

name: Pip feels dreadfully frightened
source_quote: I was dreadfully frightened

name: Pip feels giddy
source_quote: and so giddy

name: Pip clings to the man with both hands
source_quote: that I clung to him with both hands
time: when he is frightened and giddy

name: Pip politely asks to be allowed to keep upright
source_quote: and said, “If you would kindly please to let me keep upright, sir

name: Pip explains that uprightness might prevent sickness and improve attention
source_quote: perhaps I shouldn’t be sick, and perhaps I could attend more.”
time: in the same speech requesting to stand upright

name: Man dips and rolls the narrator
source_quote: He gave me a most tremendous dip and roll, so that the church jumped over its own weathercock.
location: on the top of the stone near the church

name: Man holds narrator upright by the arms
source_quote: Then, he held me by the arms, in an upright position on the top of the stone
location: on the top of the stone

name: Man continues speaking fearfully
source_quote: and went on in these fearful terms:—
location: on the top of the stone

name: Man orders boy to bring file and food
source_quote: “You bring me, to-morrow morning early, that file and them wittles.
location: on the top of the stone

name: Man specifies location of rendezvous at the old Battery
source_quote: You bring the lot to me, at that old Battery over yonder.

name: Man commands boy to stay silent about him
source_quote: you never dare to say a word or dare to make a sign concerning your having seen such a person as me
location: on the top of the stone

name: Man threatens to kill boy if he disobeys
source_quote: You fail, or you go from my words in any partickler...and your heart and your liver shall be tore out, roasted, and ate.
location: on the top of the stone

name: Man asserts he is not alone
source_quote: Now, I ain’t alone, as you may think I am.
location: on the top of the stone

name: Man claims a young man is hidden with him
source_quote: There’s a young man hid with me

name: Man compares himself favorably to the young man
source_quote: in comparison with which young man I am a Angel.
location: on the top of the stone

name: Man states young man hears his words
source_quote: That young man hears the words I speak.

name: Man describes young man’s peculiar way of getting at a boy
source_quote: That young man has a secret way pecooliar to himself, of getting at a boy, and at his heart, and at his liver.
location: on the top of the stone

name: Man claims hiding from young man is in vain
source_quote: It is in wain for a boy to attempt to hide himself from that young man.

name: Man imagines boy locking door and going to bed
source_quote: A boy may lock his door, may be warm in bed, may tuck himself up, may draw the clothes over his head

name: Man asserts young man will creep to the boy and tear him open
source_quote: but that young man will softly creep and creep his way to him and tear him open.

name: Man claims he is restraining young man from harming boy
source_quote: I am a keeping that young man from harming of you at the present moment, with great difficulty.
location: at the present moment, near the stone

name: Man states difficulty in holding young man off
source_quote: I find it wery hard to hold that young man off of your inside.

name: Man demands boy’s response
source_quote: Now, what do you say?”
location: on the top of the stone

name: Narrator promises to get the file
source_quote: I said that I would get him the file
location: on the top of the stone

name: Narrator promises to get broken bits of food
source_quote: and I would get him what broken bits of food I could
location: on the top of the stone

name: Narrator promises to come to the Battery in the morning
source_quote: and I would come to him at the Battery, early in the morning.

name: Man commands narrator to swear an oath
source_quote: “Say Lord strike you dead if you don’t!” said the man.
location: on the top of the stone

name: Man reminds listener of undertaking and orders them home
source_quote: “Now,” he pursued, “you remember what you’ve undertook, and you remember that young man, and you get home!”
location: on the top of the stone

name: Narrator is reminded of what he undertook
source_quote: “you remember what you’ve undertook”
location: on the top of the stone

name: Narrator is reminded of the young man
source_quote: “and you remember that young man”
location: on the top of the stone

name: Man exclaims dismissively 'Much of that!'
source_quote: “Much of that!” said he

name: Man glances about over the cold wet flat
source_quote: glancing about him over the cold wet flat

name: Man wishes he were a frog
source_quote: “I wish I was a frog.

name: Man wishes he were an eel
source_quote: Or a eel!”

name: Man hugs his shuddering body
source_quote: he hugged his shuddering body in both his arms

name: Man clasps himself as if to hold himself together
source_quote: clasping himself, as if to hold himself together

name: Man limps towards the low church wall
source_quote: and limped towards the low church wall

name: Narrator sees man go, picking his way among nettles and brambles
source_quote: As I saw him go, picking his way among the nettles, and among the brambles
location: near the low church wall among the nettles and brambles

name: Man picks his way among nettles and brambles
source_quote: picking his way among the nettles, and among the brambles that bound the green mounds
time: As he goes towards the wall
location: among the nettles and brambles that bound the green mounds

name: Narrator perceives man as eluding hands of dead people
source_quote: he looked in my young eyes as if he were eluding the hands of the dead people
location: near the graves by the green mounds

name: Narrator imagines dead people stretching up from graves
source_quote: dead people, stretching up cautiously out of their graves, to get a twist upon his ankle and pull him in
location: their graves by the green mounds

name: Man comes to the low church wall
source_quote: When he came to the low church wall
time: When he came to the low church wall

name: Man gets over the church wall like one with numbed legs
source_quote: he got over it, like a man whose legs were numbed and stiff
time: When he came to the low church wall

name: Man turns round to look for the narrator
source_quote: and then turned round to look for me
location: just beyond the low church wall

name: Narrator sees the man turning
source_quote: When I saw him turning

name: Narrator sets his face towards home
source_quote: I set my face towards home

name: Narrator makes the best use of his legs (hurries homeward)
source_quote: and made the best use of my legs

name: Narrator looks over his shoulder
source_quote: But presently I looked over my shoulder

name: Narrator sees man going on towards the river
source_quote: and saw him going on again towards the river
location: towards the river across the marshes

name: Man continues hugging himself in both arms while walking
source_quote: still hugging himself in both arms
time: as he goes on again towards the river
location: towards the river across the marshes

name: Man picks his way with sore feet among great stones
source_quote: picking his way with his sore feet among the great stones dropped into the marshes
time: as he goes on towards the river
location: among the great stones dropped into the marshes

name: Narrator stops to look after the man
source_quote: as I stopped to look after him
location: on the marshes overlooking river and marsh line

name: Narrator visually perceives marshes as a long black horizontal line
source_quote: The marshes were just a long black horizontal line then

name: Narrator visually perceives river as another horizontal line
source_quote: and the river was just another horizontal line, not nearly so broad nor yet so black

name: Narrator visually perceives sky as angry red and dense black lines
source_quote: and the sky was just a row of long angry red lines and dense black lines intermixed

name: Narrator faintly perceives two black upright things by the river
source_quote: On the edge of the river I could faintly make out the only two black things in all the prospect that seemed to be standing upright
location: on the edge of the river

name: Narrator identifies one object as the beacon
source_quote: one of these was the beacon by which the sailors steered
location: on the edge of the river

name: Narrator evaluates the beacon as ugly up close
source_quote: like an unhooped cask upon a pole,—an ugly thing when you were near it
location: near the beacon by the river

name: Narrator identifies the other object as a gibbet with chains
source_quote: the other, a gibbet, with some chains hanging to it which had once held a pirate
location: on the edge of the river

name: Man limps toward the gibbet
source_quote: The man was limping on towards this latter
location: from near the river toward the gibbet

name: Narrator imagines man as pirate come to life and returning to gibbet
source_quote: as if he were the pirate come to life, and come down, and going back to hook himself up again
time: while watching the man limp toward the gibbet
location: on the edge of the river, looking toward the gibbet

name: Narrator experiences a terrible shock from this thought
source_quote: It gave me a terrible turn when I thought so
time: immediately after imagining the man as the pirate
location: on the edge of the river

name: Narrator perceives cattle lifting their heads to gaze at the man
source_quote: as I saw the cattle lifting their heads to gaze after him
location: near the river, within sight of the gibbet

name: Cattle gaze after the man
source_quote: the cattle lifting their heads to gaze after him

name: Narrator wonders if cattle share his thought about the man being the pirate
source_quote: I wondered whether they thought so too
location: on the edge of the river

name: Narrator looks around for the horrible young man
source_quote: I looked all round for the horrible young man
location: on the edge of the river and surrounding area

name: Narrator fails to see any sign of the horrible young man
source_quote: and could see no signs of him

name: Narrator becomes frightened again
source_quote: But now I was frightened again
time: after failing to find the horrible young man

name: Narrator runs home without stopping
source_quote: and ran home without stopping
location: from the river area to home
